metadata.title: NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 Security Control Catalog (Abbreviated)
metadata.note: Abbreviated catalog with key controls. Full catalog available at csrc.nist.gov

AC-1.title: Policy and Procedures
AC-1.description: Develop, document, and disseminate access control policy and procedures.

AC-2.title: Account Management
AC-2.description: Manage information system accounts, including establishing, activating, modifying, reviewing, disabling, and removing accounts.

AC-2(1).title: Account Management | Automated System Account Management
AC-2(1).description: Support the management of system accounts using automated mechanisms.

AC-2(12).title: Account Management | Account Monitoring for Atypical Usage
AC-2(12).description: Monitor and report atypical usage of information system accounts.

AC-3.title: Access Enforcement
AC-3.description: Enforce approved authorizations for logical access to the system.

AC-4.title: Information Flow Enforcement
AC-4.description: Enforce approved authorizations for controlling the flow of information.

AC-5.title: Separation of Duties
AC-5.description: Separate duties of individuals to reduce the risk of malevolent activity.

AC-6.title: Least Privilege
AC-6.description: Employ the principle of least privilege, allowing only authorized accesses.

AC-6(1).title: Least Privilege | Authorize Access to Security Functions
AC-6(1).description: Authorize access to security functions and security-relevant information only to designated personnel.

AC-6(5).title: Least Privilege | Privileged Accounts
AC-6(5).description: Restrict privileged accounts to designated personnel or roles.

AC-17.title: Remote Access
AC-17.description: Establish and document usage restrictions, configuration/connection requirements, and implement policies for remote access.

AC-17(2).title: Remote Access | Protection of Confidentiality and Integrity Using Encryption
AC-17(2).description: Implement cryptographic mechanisms to protect the confidentiality and integrity of remote access sessions.

AU-1.title: Policy and Procedures
AU-1.description: Develop, document, and disseminate audit and accountability policy and procedures.

AU-2.title: Event Logging
AU-2.description: Identify the types of events that the system is capable of logging and coordinate with other organizations.

AU-3.title: Content of Audit Records
AU-3.description: Ensure audit records contain sufficient information to establish what occurred, when, where, and by whom.

AU-6.title: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting
AU-6.description: Review and analyze system audit records for indications of inappropriate or unusual activity.

AU-9.title: Protection of Audit Information
AU-9.description: Protect audit information and tools from unauthorized access, modification, and deletion.

AU-11.title: Audit Record Retention
AU-11.description: Retain audit records for a defined period to provide support for after-the-fact investigations.

AU-12.title: Audit Record Generation
AU-12.description: Provide audit record generation capability for the list of events defined in AU-2.

CA-2.title: Control Assessments
CA-2.description: Assess the controls in the system on a defined frequency to determine if the controls are implemented correctly.

CA-3.title: Information Exchange
CA-3.description: Approve and manage the exchange of information between the system and other systems.

CA-5.title: Plan of Action and Milestones
CA-5.description: Develop a POA&M for the system to document the planned remediation actions.

CA-6.title: Authorization
CA-6.description: Assign a senior official as the authorizing official, ensure the official authorizes the system before operating.

CA-7.title: Continuous Monitoring
CA-7.description: Develop a system-level continuous monitoring strategy and implement continuous monitoring program.

CA-8.title: Penetration Testing
CA-8.description: Conduct penetration testing on the system on a defined frequency.

CA-9.title: Internal System Connections
CA-9.description: Authorize internal connections of system components to the system.

CM-2.title: Baseline Configuration
CM-2.description: Develop, document, and maintain a current baseline configuration of the system.

CM-3.title: Configuration Change Control
CM-3.description: Determine the types of changes to the system that are configuration-controlled.

CM-5.title: Access Restrictions for Change
CM-5.description: Define, document, approve, and enforce physical and logical access restrictions associated with changes.

CM-6.title: Configuration Settings
CM-6.description: Establish and document configuration settings using security configuration checklists (STIGs).

CM-7.title: Least Functionality
CM-7.description: Configure the system to provide only essential capabilities, prohibiting or restricting the use of functions, ports, protocols, software, and services not required.

CM-8.title: System Component Inventory
CM-8.description: Develop and document an inventory of system components that accurately reflects the current system.

CM-10.title: Software Usage Restrictions
CM-10.description: Use software and associated documentation in accordance with licensing agreements.

CM-14.title: Signed Components
CM-14.description: Prevent the installation of software without verification that the component is signed using a certificate recognized and approved by the organization.

CP-1.title: Policy and Procedures
CP-1.description: Develop, document, and disseminate contingency planning policy and procedures.

CP-2.title: Contingency Plan
CP-2.description: Develop a contingency plan for the system that identifies essential missions and business functions.

CP-4.title: Contingency Plan Testing
CP-4.description: Test the contingency plan for the system on a defined frequency.

CP-6.title: Alternate Storage Site
CP-6.description: Establish an alternate storage site for the system including necessary agreements.

CP-9.title: System Backup
CP-9.description: Conduct backups of user-level information, system-level information, and system documentation.

CP-10.title: System Recovery and Reconstitution
CP-10.description: Provide for the recovery and reconstitution of the system to a known state after disruption.

IA-2.title: Identification and Authentication (Organizational Users)
IA-2.description: Uniquely identify and authenticate organizational users.

IA-2(1).title: MFA to Privileged Accounts
IA-2(1).description: Implement MFA for access to privileged accounts.

IA-2(2).title: MFA to Non-Privileged Accounts
IA-2(2).description: Implement MFA for access to non-privileged accounts.

IA-2(12).title: Acceptance of PIV Credentials
IA-2(12).description: Accept and electronically verify Personal Identity Verification (PIV) credentials.

IA-4.title: Identifier Management
IA-4.description: Manage system identifiers by receiving authorization from designated official to assign identifier.

IA-5.title: Authenticator Management
IA-5.description: Manage system authenticators including verifying identity of individual/device receiving authenticator.

IA-5(1).title: Authenticator Management | Password-Based Authentication
IA-5(1).description: Enforce password complexity, expiration, reuse restrictions, and temporary initial password change on first use.

IA-5(2).title: Authenticator Management | Public Key-Based Authentication
IA-5(2).description: Require the validation of PKI certificates.

IA-7.title: Cryptographic Module Authentication
IA-7.description: Implement mechanisms for authentication to a cryptographic module that meet requirements of applicable federal laws for such authentication.

IA-8.title: Identification and Authentication (Non-Organizational Users)
IA-8.description: Uniquely identify and authenticate non-organizational users.

IR-4.title: Incident Handling
IR-4.description: Implement an incident handling capability for incidents including preparation, detection and analysis, containment, eradication, and recovery.

IR-6.title: Incident Reporting
IR-6.description: Require personnel to report suspected incidents to the incident response capability within a defined time period.

IR-8.title: Incident Response Plan
IR-8.description: Develop and implement an incident response plan.

RA-3.title: Risk Assessment
RA-3.description: Conduct a risk assessment for the system.

RA-5.title: Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning
RA-5.description: Monitor and scan for vulnerabilities in the system on a defined frequency.

RA-5(2).title: Vulnerability Scanning | Update Vulnerabilities to Be Scanned
RA-5(2).description: Update the system vulnerabilities scanned before each scan and when new vulnerabilities are identified.

RA-5(5).title: Vulnerability Scanning | Privileged Access
RA-5(5).description: Implement privileged access authorization to operating systems, middleware, and applications for vulnerability scanning.

SA-10.title: Developer Configuration Management
SA-10.description: Require developers to perform configuration management during development.

SA-11.title: Developer Testing and Evaluation
SA-11.description: Require developers to create and implement security assessment plan, perform security testing/evaluation.

SA-15.title: Development Process, Standards, and Tools
SA-15.description: Require developer to follow a documented development process that explicitly addresses security requirements.

SC-5.title: Denial-of-Service Protection
SC-5.description: Protect against or limit the effects of denial-of-service attacks.

SC-7.title: Boundary Protection
SC-7.description: Monitor and control communications at the external boundary and key internal boundaries.

SC-8.title: Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity
SC-8.description: Implement cryptographic mechanisms to prevent unauthorized disclosure during transmission.

SC-8(1).title: Transmission Confidentiality | Cryptographic Protection
SC-8(1).description: Implement cryptographic mechanisms to prevent unauthorized disclosure of information and detect changes during transmission.

SC-12.title: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management
SC-12.description: Establish and manage cryptographic keys for required cryptography.

SC-13.title: Cryptographic Protection
SC-13.description: Determine the cryptographic uses required and implement FIPS 140-validated cryptographic modules.

SC-28.title: Protection of Information at Rest
SC-28.description: Protect the confidentiality and integrity of information at rest.

SC-28(1).title: Protection of Information at Rest | Cryptographic Protection
SC-28(1).description: Implement cryptographic mechanisms to prevent unauthorized disclosure and modification of information at rest.

SC-39.title: Process Isolation
SC-39.description: Maintain a separate execution domain for each executing process.

SI-2.title: Flaw Remediation
SI-2.description: Identify, report, and correct system flaws; test software updates related to flaw remediation.

SI-3.title: Malicious Code Protection
SI-3.description: Implement malicious code protection mechanisms at system entry/exit points.

SI-4.title: System Monitoring
SI-4.description: Monitor the system to detect attacks, unauthorized connections, and unusual activity.

SI-6.title: Security and Privacy Function Verification
SI-6.description: Verify the correct operation of security and privacy functions.

SI-7.title: Software, Firmware, and Information Integrity
SI-7.description: Employ integrity verification tools to detect unauthorized changes to software, firmware, and information.

SI-7(1).title: Software Integrity | Integrity Checks
SI-7(1).description: Perform an integrity check of software on a defined frequency and upon startup, on-demand.

SI-7(6).title: Software Integrity | Cryptographic Protection
SI-7(6).description: Implement cryptographic mechanisms to detect unauthorized changes to software, firmware, and information.

SR-1.title: Policy and Procedures
SR-1.description: Develop and disseminate supply chain risk management policy.

SR-2.title: Supply Chain Risk Management Plan
SR-2.description: Develop and update a plan for managing supply chain risks.

SR-3.title: Supply Chain Controls and Processes
SR-3.description: Establish a process to identify and address weaknesses in supply chain.

SR-5.title: Acquisition Strategies, Tools, and Methods
SR-5.description: Employ acquisition strategies, contract tools, and procurement methods to protect against supply chain threats.

SR-11.title: Component Authenticity
SR-11.description: Develop and implement anti-counterfeit policy and procedures to detect counterfeit components.
